decision-tree.root-question: Are kill-criteria pre-registered AND is the eval set frozen AND is a cost ceiling set?

branch.when: kill_criteria_committed == true AND eval_set_frozen == true AND cost_ceiling_set == true
branch.question: Was the judge-LLM recalibrated within the last 90 days?

branch.conclusion: Run the eval. Emit n + mean + CI per comparison. Apply r1-r2-r5 to enforce pre-registered thresholds, frozen set, and bounded cost.
Block. Recalibrate the judge against ≥20 human-labeled examples before running the eval — stale judge invalidates the result.
Block. Write and commit pass / fail / freeze thresholds BEFORE any eval run — post-hoc thresholds invite rationalisation.
Block. Pin and version the ground-truth set before this comparison — mid-comparison additions invalidate the metric.
Block. Define the per-run cost ceiling (≤ 2× daily production traffic cost) before running — unbounded eval budgets break the cost case.

example.p: Kill criteria committed (pass-rate ≥ 0.80). Set golden-v3 frozen at 247 examples. Cost ceiling $80/run. Judge recalibrated 30 days ago. → conclusion r3-statistical-reporting → run + emit n + mean + CI.
